title: NOC 41101 – Lawyers and Quebec Notaries | GTR Immigration
description: Lawyers and Quebec Notaries under NOC 41101 are highly trained legal professionals who advise clients, represent them before courts and tribunals, and draft legally binding documents across Canada. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role is central to Canada’s justice system and business environment. Legal professionals under this NOC work in law firms, corporate legal departments, government agencies, and courts. In Quebec, notaries operate under civil law traditions, while lawyers in other provinces practice under common law systems.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in lawSub-Major Group411 – Professional occupations in lawMinor Group4110 – Judges, lawyers and Quebec notariesNOC Code41101

duties: Lawyers and Quebec Notaries perform some or all of the following duties:
Advise clients on legal rights and obligations
Research case law and gather evidence
Represent clients before courts, tribunals, and boards (lawyers)
Draft contracts, wills, divorce agreements, and real estate documents
Perform administrative and law practice management duties
Act as mediator, conciliator, or arbitrator
Act as executor, trustee, or guardian in estate matters
employment_requirements: 2–3 years of undergraduate studies (or Quebec college completion)
Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) or Juris Doctor (J.D.) from a recognized law school
Licensing by provincial or territorial law society
Active membership in the law society of practice
Complete National Committee on Accreditation (NCA) requirements
Be called to the bar in a Canadian province
Master’s degree in notarial law (includes 16-week internship)
15-day training program at the Chambre des notaires du Québec
Licensing by the Chambre des notaires du Québec
salary_prospects: Legal professionals under NOC 41101 typically earn between:
$37,400 – $294,400 per year, depending on jurisdiction, specialization, seniority, and firm structure.
Senior partners and corporate counsel may earn significantly higher compensation.
job_outlook: The outlook for Lawyers and Quebec Notaries remains stable and competitive due to:
Competition is strong, particularly in large metropolitan areas.
labour_market_demand: Lawyers and Quebec Notaries are in demand across:
Real estate and estate planning sectors
Population growth, increased regulatory complexity, immigration matters, and commercial expansion continue to drive legal service demand nationwide.
High social prestige and professional recognition
Long-term pathway to senior legal roles (e.g., partner, Crown attorney)
However, practicing law in Canada requires provincial licensing. Foreign-trained lawyers must complete credential assessments and meet provincial bar admission requirements.
